title: Broken Markets
subtitle: How Technology and Predatory Practices Are Destroying Investor Confidence
author: Kevin McPartland
authorBio: Kevin McPartland is Head of Research for Market Structure and Technology at Coalition Greenwich (a CRISIL company). He has over 25 years of capital markets experience and is a leading authority on electronic trading, market structure, and the impact of technology on financial markets. He previously held roles at BlackRock and has advised regulators and exchanges globally.
keyConcepts: Dark pools and lit market interaction
notableQuotes: The markets are not broken in the sense of being dysfunctional. They are broken in the sense of being unfair.
Technology has transformed markets faster than regulation can adapt.
The biggest threat to market health is not HFT itself — it is the perception that markets are rigged.
We have built the most technologically advanced market in history, but we forgot to make it fair.
